title: pi-workflows discoverability surface is weak — workflows live in ~/.pi/workflows/ or .pi/workflows/ + execute via workflow-execute tool or TUI picker; Claude Code workflows auto-register as /<name> slash commands + appear in / autocomplete; pi has no equivalent runtime-discovery / autocomplete / slash-command-shorthand surface
description: Per R-0014 recon axis 11: Claude Code workflows registered at .claude/workflows/<name>.js appear as /<name> slash commands in / autocomplete; discoverable via /workflows list. pi-workflows lives in directories but execution requires either workflow-execute tool invocation (with explicit name argument) or TUI picker navigation. No slash-command-shorthand registration; no auto-complete-in-conversation surface; no /workflows-list-style command on the pi side. Operators running frequently-used workflows incur friction every time.

evidence.reference: Part 3 axis 11 — Bundled / Discoverable Workflows — INSPIRE verdict
workflow-execute / workflow-list / workflow-status tools but no slash-command auto-registration

impact: Operator friction on frequently-used workflows. Workflow discovery requires explicit knowledge of names + invocation conventions. Mapping to Claude-Code-side parity for cross-system operator UX is poor — slash-command-shorthand is the canonical pi-extension command surface but not extended to workflows.
canonical_vocabulary: slash-command registration; workflow autocomplete; pi-side / autocomplete equivalent; bundled-workflow surface parity
proposed_resolution: Extend pi-agent-dispatch or pi-workflows extension to register each discovered workflow as a pi slash command. Auto-discovery from bundled (packages/*/workflows/) + project (.pi/workflows/) + user (~/.pi/workflows/) directories at extension load. Slash command shape: /<workflow-name> invokes workflow-execute with that name + parses args per the workflow's inputSchema. TUI: add workflow picker to main menu (alongside other actions). Documentation refresh for discoverability + invocation. Verify pi CLI supports dynamic slash-command registration (may require upstream surface; if not, file follow-on FGAP).
